ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sales_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp for the forecasted period */,
  "trend" REAL /* Predicted trend value */,
  "yhat_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the prediction interval */,
  "yhat_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the prediction interval */,
  "trend_lower" REAL /* Lower bound for the trend value */,
  "trend_upper" REAL /* Upper bound for the trend value */,
  "additive_terms" REAL /* Additive terms contributing to the prediction */,
  "additive_terms_lower" REAL /* Lower bound for the additive terms */,
  "additive_terms_upper" REAL /* Upper bound for the additive terms */,
  "daily" REAL /* Daily forecast value */,
  "daily_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the daily forecast */,
  "daily_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the daily forecast */,
  "weekly" REAL /* Weekly forecast value */,
  "weekly_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the weekly forecast */,
  "weekly_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the weekly forecast */,
  "multiplicative_terms" REAL /* Multiplicative terms contributing to the prediction */,
  "multiplicative_terms_lower" REAL /* Lower bound for the multiplicative terms */,
  "multiplicative_terms_upper" REAL /* Upper bound for the multiplicative terms */,
  "yhat" REAL /* Final predicted value */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product associated with the forecast */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region associated with the forecast */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model used for the forecast */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the forecast */,
  "forecast_source" TEXT /* Source of the forecast (e.g., manual, automated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_forecasts_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_forecasts_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_forecasts_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecast_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Product category */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier of the product */,
  "reorder_point" INTEGER /* Inventory level at which the product should be reordered */,
  "lead_time" INTEGER /* Lead time for restocking the product */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update of the product details */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_data" (
  "sales_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales record */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product sold */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the sale */,
  "units_sold" INTEGER /* Number of units sold */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Total revenue generated from the sale */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region where the sale occurred */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store where the sale occurred */,
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* ID of the promotion associated with the sale */,
  "return_quantity" INTEGER /* Number of units returned */,
  "return_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the return */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sales_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_data_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country in which the region is located */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the region */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency used in the region */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update of the region details */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_forecast_regions" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecast */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region associated with the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id", "region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the forecast model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., additive, multiplicative) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the model was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the model */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update of the model details */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for performance record */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecast model */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated forecast */,
  "mse" REAL /* Mean squared error of the forecast */,
  "mae" REAL /* Mean absolute error of the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecast_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES sales_forecasts ("forecast_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* Profile picture of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, update) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's access */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Session ID of the user's access */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
